entry.title: Re: Free Goods Determination not getting determined in Sa...
entry.content:
Hello @thangarajthomas 

As per the standard, if you want to use the free goods determination process, certain business conditions need to be met:

  • The material must be available in stock.
  • If you are using trading goods, you can directly procure the material

Additionally, from a configuration perspective, the item category determination for free goods is based on usage and the higher-level item category, which cannot be edited in the current version.

Ideally, free goods are not applicable for make-to-order products. 

However, it would be great if you could explain your business requirement.

Thank You

entry.title: How to restrict JNL delivery document in open billing documents list for intra-state STO process
entry.content:
Hello Experts, 

Could you please advise on the option to restrict JNL delivery document in the open billing documents list for Intra-State STO process in India. .

We are using SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud.

In India, for STO transactions within state (Intra-State - same region code for plant and customer ), there is no requirement to create billing document. 

However for Inter-state STO transactions (different region code for plant and customer), billing document is required. 

Our requirement is to restrict JNL delivery document (for Intra-state) in VF04 billing due list. 

Could you please advise if there is any BaDI or any other enhancement option available in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud,  to enable this restriction based on same region code for Plant & customer.

VF04 - Maintain Billing Due List

Please advise. 

Thank you

kind regards

Thangaraj Thomas

entry.title: Unlocking Value: Insights from the SAP ALM Summit 2024 EMEA
entry.content:
Anticipation for the SAP ALM Summit EMEA in Mannheim, Germany, had been growing for weeks, and the event week finally arrived. The day before, the Manufaktur venue buzzed with activity as banners were hung, booths were set up, and excitement filled the air. Speakers conducted dry runs to ensure thorough preparation to deliver the best experience for customers and partners. 

When the big day finally arrived, the venue came alive with activity. The attendees started to trickle in, greeted warmly by smiling faces at the registration desk. The aroma of fresh coffee filled the air, providing a comforting backdrop to the keynote sessions. 

The keynotes featured an exciting and fresh approach delivered by multiple industry experts from SAP, SAP partners and customers. The audience quickly became engaged, sitting up, leaning in, and reaching for their phones to capture the moment. 

From the beginning, speakers used various metaphors to explain SAP Cloud ALM, such as “Constant Companion,” and “Guiding Light” which captivated the audience. Explaining a data migration strategy for transitioning from SAP Solution Manager to SAP Cloud ALM on a single napkin is challenging, yet we successfully captured the participants' attention, leaving them eager for more. Our RISE with SAP and Clean Core team colleagues helped us connect the dots regarding SAP’s strategy and how SAP Cloud ALM is the key adoption platform for our customers’ transformations. You will soon find the inspiring session recordings and presentations on our event page. 

 The time is NOW to start planning your transition! 

This Manufaktur venue brought a refreshing, youthful energy to the event. An old cotton ball factory transformed into a modern event space, its industrial chic style embodies the idea of transformation, perfectly encapsulating the occasion. Attendees learned how they can benefit from SAP Cloud ALM for their SAP S/4HANA transition projects and how to align with clean core principles. They also learned how SAP recognizes that transitioning from the cherished SAP Solution Manager to SAP Cloud ALM can be challenging for many customers, but they will be supported with tools and content that will enable them to adopt SAP’s essential ALM solution for the future. Several sessions covered the planned transition methodology roadmap and selected data transition of content from SAP Solution Manager to SAP Cloud ALM, and customer feedback was positive. 

SAP Cloud ALM APIs 

Attendees experienced real-world examples from our partners and customers that demonstrated how they have consumed, extended, integrated, and complemented their ALM processes. Watching these use cases is a testament to the openness and limitless innovation in the SAP ALM world. One example that captured my attention was the bi-directional Jira to SAP Cloud ALM integration that will evolve into a business-process-based test automation approach for the future. 

SAP Partner NTT “conquered the world” with their winning ALM-athon demonstration!

 The SAP ALM Summit EMEA was more than just an event; it was a collaborative effort where all attendees played a crucial role. Partners showcased their latest solutions, customers shared their success stories, and the ALM community celebrated the ALM-athon winner NTT. Having been part of the SAP ecosystem for two decades, the sense of community keeps me deeply connected.  

Thanks to everyone for all the fulfilling conversations, learning, and sense of belonging, and see you soon at SAP ALM SUMMIT North America at SAP Headquarters (Newtown Square PA) from March 24-26! 

PS: I’ll update this post with the link to the event recordings when it’s available. 

End of the event Celebration

entry.title: Re: SCC1N Usage - Simple Way
entry.content:
Just some comments for the audience reading this topic.  This great explanation by Gopagani  is available for SAP S/4HANA on-premise and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition.

On SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition this transaction is not available as this is a standard solution and copy of transports into different clients is not possible as you can't create new clients outside of what is delivered standard in a 3 system landscape (Dev (client 100, 80) - Test (100) and Production (100)). In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition in a 3 system landscape, you can off course move transports into the customer system landscape  - Development, Testing and Production. We will also offering a new concept called Parallel Project Line, which will allow our customers to create another line to create additional clients in Development (120) and Testing (120) which they can be used to run testing, data migration example, etc. so you don't do these changes in the main line.  If interested in this concept I suggest to check the following information: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/bee2fd8498b5420696cdca00a83b2ba6/ba3c2949cab04efbb7136bcab7e69ff0.html?q=Parallel%20Project%20Line&locale=en-US

Hope this helps

entry.title: Re: A new generation of CDS views: CDS view entities
entry.content:
Hi AndreaUS,

we have kind of strange behaviour with view entities which lead to some trouble for us. I distilled the essence in this example. It occurs on our S/4 HANA 2022 system.

Given this DDIC based CDS-View

 

 

@AbapCatalog.sqlViewName: 'ZTESTDDICCDS'
@AbapCatalog.compiler.compareFilter: true
@AbapCatalog.preserveKey: true
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'test cds'
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true
define view ztest_ddic_cds
  as select from t000
{
  key mandt,
      mtext,
      mwaer,

      case when t000.mwaer = 'CHF'
        then 'X'
        else ''
      end as is_chf,

      case when t000.mwaer = 'CHF'
      then 'X'
      else ' '
      end as is_chf2
}

 

 

gives this SQL CREATE statement

 

 

CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW "ZTESTDDICCDS" AS SELECT 
  "T000"."MANDT", 
  "T000"."MTEXT", 
  "T000"."MWAER", 
  CASE WHEN "T000"."MWAER" = N'CHF' THEN N'X' ELSE N'' 
  END AS "IS_CHF", 
  CASE WHEN "T000"."MWAER" = N'CHF' THEN N'X' ELSE N'' 
  END AS "IS_CHF2" 
FROM "T000" "T000"

 

 

As a view entity it looks like this:

 

 

@AbapCatalog.viewEnhancementCategory: [#NONE]
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'test cds'
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true
@ObjectModel.usageType:{
  serviceQuality: #X,
  sizeCategory: #S,
  dataClass: #MIXED
}
define view entity ztest_cds
  as select from t000
{
  key mandt,
      mtext,
      mwaer,

      case when t000.mwaer = 'CHF'
        then 'X'
        else ''
      end as is_chf,

      case when t000.mwaer = 'CHF'
      then 'X'
      else ' '
      end as is_chf2
}

 

 

 gives this SQL CREATE statement

 

 

CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW "ZTEST_CDS" AS SELECT 
  "T000"."MANDT", 
  "T000"."MTEXT", 
  "T000"."MWAER", 
  CASE WHEN "T000"."MWAER" = N'CHF' THEN N'X' ELSE N'' 
  END AS "IS_CHF", 
  CASE WHEN "T000"."MWAER" = N'CHF' THEN N'X' ELSE NCHAR(32) 
  END AS "IS_CHF2" 
FROM "T000" "T000"

 

 

As you can see in line 7 the ELSE branch has type NCHAR(32). Now the question is why? This is completely unexpected to us and lead to some trouble in above layers.

Thanks in advance.

BR Christian

entry.title: How the SAP's 'Aspiring Product Manager' Program Helped Me Thrive in My Role
entry.content:
Bridging the Gap: From Technical to Strategic

As a former cloud architect, my focus had always been on technical design, implementation, and ensuring systems were scalable and robust. However, as a product manager, I learned that my primary focus should be on the customer—understanding their needs, behaviors, and pain points. This shift in perspective is something the training program helped me cultivate.

The structured learning sessions exposed me to crucial concepts such as user empathy, strategic roadmapping, and data-driven decision-making. It allowed me to see the bigger picture beyond just solving technical problems: I had to learn how to think about why a product is being built and how it delivers value to the customer.

Key Product Management Skills I Developed

  1. Customer-Centric Thinking
    One of the most significant changes in my mindset was learning to prioritize the customer journey. As part of the training, we dove deep into creating personas, mapping customer journeys, and understanding how to anticipate user needs. This was instrumental when I was tasked with revamping customer journey maps and personas for a greenfield 0-to-1 app, aimed at reducing the time to value (TTV) for our customers from months to minutes.

  2. Defining Product Strategy
    Coming from an architect’s background, I was comfortable with technical design, but I had little experience in defining product vision and strategy. The training covered how to craft a product vision, aligning it with business objectives, and how to make data-informed decisions. These skills are now at the core of my role within the ISBN product strategy team. I’m involved in redesigning the deployment experience of the ISBN suite, which includes automation and self-service features. The training empowered me to contribute more effectively to high-level product discussions.

  3. Cross-functional Collaboration
    Working with diverse teams was not new to me, but leading cross-functional collaboration as a product manager is a different challenge altogether. The training highlighted best practices for communication and collaboration with engineering, UX, sales, marketing, and customer support teams. This has been vital in my current role where I lead initiatives involving multiple stakeholders.

  4. Prioritization and Decision-Making
    With so many moving parts in a product's development, one of the toughest skills to master is prioritization. The program taught us frameworks like the RICE score model (Reach, Impact, Confidence, Effort), which have helped me make informed decisions when balancing customer needs with business goals. This has been especially valuable in launching new features for the ISBN suite, where timelines are tight and resources limited.

The Outcome: Delivering Greater Impact

Since completing the Aspiring Product Manager program, I have been able to approach my responsibilities with a renewed focus. Whether it's defining the product strategy for ISBN or working on a greenfield application, the skills I honed during the training have allowed me to have a greater impact. I’ve gained the confidence to make strategic decisions and now have a clearer understanding of how to lead products that solve real customer problems.

The training also gave me the tools to understand the business side of product management, which was something I hadn’t previously had the opportunity to explore in-depth. This is crucial as we continue to innovate in SAP, and especially as we design new, automated solutions to enhance customer experiences with our products.

Final Thoughts

For anyone with a technical background looking to transition into product management, I can’t recommend the Aspiring Product Manager program enough(available internally only for SAP employees!). It not only provided me with the skills to thrive in my current role but also changed how I approach product challenges—thinking more strategically and always with the customer in mind. My journey as a product manager is just getting started, but with this foundational training, I feel more prepared than ever to take on new challenges and continue driving product success at SAP.

entry.title: Re: Emissão Cloud MDFe Oubound campo issuingDateTime
entry.content:
Olá Bruno, tudo bem?

Essa data/hora deve estar no formato "ISO 8601 Date/Time with time zone", ou seja, "yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss+|–hh:mm", exemplo "2020-07-30T15:30:12-03:00".

Na documentação do SAP Business Accelerator Hub também tem esse exemplo. Para acessar, expanda o campo na setinha à direita. ()

Uma boa opção seria usar o método GET_DATE_TIME_WITH_TIMEZONE da classe CL_NFE_CLOUD_DATE_TIME_FORMAT (entregue na SAP Note 3111469) para formatar uma data/hora/timezone do ABAP.

Atenciosamente,
Gideão Franco.

entry.title: Re: How to Create Custom CDS View of Customer request
entry.content:
Well, CDS is not required in this case. The utility of CDS is to fetch data from sap tables which is not directly exposed in S/4HC. To create the interface, you need to find the appropriate API from the API hub. The API documentation will have the details about required fields. The mapping between the external fields and the s/4 fidls needs to be done using CPI tool. 

Thank you!

entry.title: Unable to Create the GL account groups in the CBC
entry.content:
Dear Team, 

We are trying to create the GL account groups in the CBC. like below, but SAP is not allowing the namespace and giving the error message. 

Like example:

TURNTURNOVER1000000017999999
COSACOST OF SALES2000000029999999
ADEXADMINISTRATIVE EXPENSES3000000039999999
OPINOTHER OPERATING INCOME1800000019999999

As per the SAP note 2326112 - Namespace restriction in SSCUI, we use the name spaces other than below but still getting the error message. 

  • S, Q, R, X, P, Y for character fields
  • 5, 6, 7, 8, 9, 0 for numeric fields

Question: Is there any restrictions on the name space in the 2408 version? Should we only use Z or Y name space for the account groups?

Regards

Naveen

entry.title: Re: how to add a field in ALV of transaction IW47
entry.content: Got it. You mean that you have changed RIHAFVR because it's the structure of the lines of the ALV internal table, then it means that it's in the ALV. Now, you are saying that by debug you can see that IW47 considers your field (it's in the ALV field catalog) but it's not displayed.

entry.title: Re: how to add a field in ALV of transaction IW47
entry.content: If the new field is part of the internal table and of the field catalog, but it's not displayed, it means that the field catalog has TECH='X' or it's using the ALV buffer, so you need to bypass it or force all ALV buffer to be reloaded (program BALVBUFDEL).

entry.title: Re: Custom fields for special requests in Learning
entry.content: As I understand, even though the user was inactivated, the special request data will still be displayed. If you purge the user or remove its registration, then the special request data should be gone.

entry.title: Creating ODATA Draft Scenario for CURD Operations
entry.content:
ODATA DRAFT SCENARIOS 

 What is Draft? 

A draft is the state of the business object when its data is not persisted in the actual tables but is stored in some temporary tables which can be quickly retrieved to continue working (from the place left) in case the work is left in the middle due to some reason. Draft Management Process Flow: 

Consider an application with Sales Order (SO) Header and Item data. The data can be displayed and updated or new SOs can be created. 

Drafts play no role in Display functionality. It is when any user either enters into an update mode or a create mode drafts are involved. 

A Draft can be: 

  • Created when the user enters into update mode – all actual table data is copied for temporary tables/draft tables and then data is presented on the screen for the user to update. 
  • Created when the user enters into create mode- table entries with draft keys are created in draft tables. 
  • Updated when the user enters data in any field, changes a tab on the screen, on any clicks, the press of a button, or at a set time depending on the requirement of the application. Any one of the trigger points or a combination of these trigger points can be used to implement this part. 
  • Deleted when a draft is discarded by the user, or when the data is saved to actual tables. 

Implement Draft Management: 

Create Draft tables to store the data temporarily. In this case, draft tables will be require for Header and Item data. 

Tables for draft data: 

Steps to create Entity types: 

Go to SAP Gateway Service Builder(SEGW). 

 

Click on create project node. 

 

Provide the project name, description, package name and click on Continue. 

 

Select the Data model and Right click on data model. 

 

Select Import and click on DDIC Structures node. 

Provide the entity name and DDIC table name which table you want to import. 

Click on Next. 

Select the required fields which you want to display/perform operations. 

Click on Next. 

 

Select the atleast one key fields from that structure and click on finish. 

Create one more entity type for Item details. 

Click on Save. 

 

Click on Generate Runtime Objects node. 

 

By default/Implicitly 4 classes, one technical model and one technical service is created. 

Click on continue. 

In addition to this one more entity with just a text field is defined to be used as the return type 

Create one return type structure. 

Add one return type. 

Steps to create Association : 

Right click on Data model. 

 

Select Create and click on Association node. 

Provide the Association name, Principle name as Header entity name, Dependent name as Item entity name and provide the cardinality for displaying the item details and provide the navigation property name. 

Click on Next. 

Provide dependent property name as common field name for both header and item entity. 

Click on next. 

Click on Finish. 

Create one deep structure in MPC_EXT class. 

Provide Navigation property name for item details like as shown in below 

 

Steps to create Function Import: 

3 function imports, CreateDraft, DeleteDraft, and SaveOrder are created to achieve the functionality. All use the Return Type as the entity type Return. 

Right click on Data model. 

 

Select the create and Click on Function import. 

Provide the Function import name, Click on continue. 

Provide the return type kind as Entity type, Return type as Return entity name, pass the cardinality and select the HTTP method as post. 

Like this create 2 more function import for DeleteDraft, and SaveOrder. 

Provide the parameter for function import. 

Code Reference from ZCL_ZGR_ODATA_DRAFT_SC_DPC_EXT 

 

Read and Query Operations: 

These operations usually will refer to the actual tables/Standard BAPI etc., to get data.

 

 

method SO_HEADERSET_GET_ENTITY. 
  
    DATA : lv_vbeln TYPE vbak-vbeln. 
  
    DATA(it_keys) = io_tech_request_context->get_keys( ). 
  
    lv_vbeln = VALUE #( it_keys[ name = 'VBELN' ]-value optional ). 
     lv_vbeln = |{ lv_vbeln ALPHA = IN }|. 
  
    select SINGLE  FROM vbak 
                   FIELDS vbeln, 
                          erdat, 
                          erzet, 
                          ernam, 
                          netwr, 
                          waerk, 
                          vkorg, 
                          vtweg, 
                          spart, 
                          kunnr 
                     WHERE vbeln = _vbeln 
                   INTO CORRESPONDING FIELDS OF _entity. 
  
  endmethod.

 

 

 

 

method SO_HEADERSET_GET_ENTITYSET. 
  
    SELECT FROM vbak 
     FIELDS vbeln, erdat, erzet, ernam, netwr, 
            waerk, vkorg, vtweg, spart, kunnr 
     INTO CORRESPONDING FIELDS OF TABLE _entityset 
     UP TO 20 ROWS. 
  
  endmethod.

 

 

Read/ Get Item data: 

 

 

method SO_ITEMSET_GET_ENTITY. 
  
    DATA ls_keys TYPE zcl_zgr_odata_draft_sc_mpc=>ts_so_item. 
  
    io_tech_request_context->get_converted_keys( 
       IMPORTING 
         es_key_values = ls_keys                 " Entity Key Values - converted 
     ). 
  
     SELECT SINGLE FROM vbap 
                    FIELDS vbeln, posnr, matnr, arktx, zmeng, meins, 
                           netwr, waerk, werks, lgort, vstel, route 
                     WHERE vbeln = _keys-vbeln 
                           AND   posnr = _keys-posnr 
                     INTO CORRESPONDING FIELDS OF _entity. 
   endmethod.

 

 

 

 

METHOD so_itemset_get_entityset. 
  
    DATA lv_vbeln TYPE vbak-vbeln. 
  
    IF io_tech_request_context->get_source_entity_type_name( ) = 'SO_Header'. 
  
      DATA(It_Keys) = io_tech_request_context->get_source_keys( ). 
  
      lv_vbeln = VALUE #( it_keys[ name = 'Vbeln' ]-value OPTIONAL ). 
       lv_vbeln = |{ lv_vbeln ALPHA = IN }|. 
  
      SELECT FROM vbap 
       FIELDS vbeln, posnr, matnr, arktx, zmeng, meins, 
          netwr, waerk, werks, lgort, vstel, route 
       WHERE vbeln = _vbeln 
       INTO CORRESPONDING FIELDS OF TABLE _entityset. 
  
    ELSE. 
  
      SELECT FROM vbap 
              FIELDS vbeln, posnr, matnr, arktx, zmeng, meins, 
                     netwr, waerk, werks, lgort, vstel, route 
               INTO CORRESPONDING FIELDS OF TABLE _entityset. 
  
    ENDIF. 
  
  ENDMETHOD.

 

 

CREATE_DRAFT Method: 

An additional method is created in the DPC_EXT class with importing parameter VBELN i.e., Order Number and Exporting parameter Return which is the same type as extra entity. 

This method will check weather the draft exists for the order. If it does and it is by another user, it will return an error text. Otherwise, the method will copy the data from the standard table to custom draft tables ZGR_T_SOH_DRAFT and ZGR_T_SOI_DRAFT. 

To Keep the logic simple, the method uses incremental logic to generate a key. In an actual scenario, we will either use a hash generator or number range to create the draft key. 

Note: Create_Draft method is a custom method which is have importing and Returning parameter. 

 

 

  method CREATE_DRAFT. 
  
    "Check if draft exists 
     SELECT SINGLE FROM zgr_t_soh_draft 
                   FIELDS * 
                   INTO (ls_draft). 
  
      IF sy-subrc EQ 0. "Draft exist 
  
        IF sy-uname = ls_draft-ernam. "Draft by same user 
  
          RETURN = VALUE zgr_s_return( test = 'S Draft success' ). 
  
          ELSE. 
  
            RETURN-test = |E Draft user : { ls_draft-ernam }|. 
  
        ENDIF. 
  
      ELSE. "No Draft 
  
        SELECT SINGLE FROM vbak 
                       FIELDS vbeln, erdat, erzet, ernam, netwr, 
                              waerk, vkorg, vtweg, spart, kunnr 
                       WHERE vbeln = @vbeln 
                       INTO (ls_vbak). 
  
          IF sy-subrc EQ 0. 
  
            ls_draft = CORRESPONDING #( ls_vbak ). 
             ls_draft-ernam = sy-uname. 
  
            INSERT zgr_t_soh_draft FROM ls_draft. 
  
            IF sy-subrc EQ 0. 
  
              RETURN-test = 'S Draft Success'. 
  
              ELSE. 
                 RETURN-test = 'S Draft Failed'. 
  
 
            ENDIF. 
  
          ENDIF. 
  
          "Add items to the draft 
  
          SELECT  FROM vbap 
                  FIELDS mandt, vbeln, posnr, matnr, arktx, zmeng, meins, 
                         netwr, waerk, werks, lgort, vstel, route 
                  WHERE vbeln = @vbeln 
                  INTO TABLE (lt_items). 
  
            IF sy-subrc EQ 0. 
  
              INSERT zgr_t_soi_draft FROM TABLE _items. 
  
            ENDIF. 
  
      ENDIF. 
  
  endmethod.

 

 

CREATE Operation: 

Create Operation should be called only for item data. For header data instead of create, created draft should be called. Item create operation, actually only creates an entry in the draft table. 

 

 

method SO_ITEMSET_CREATE_ENTITY. 
  
  DATA:  ls_Final TYPE zcl_zgr_odata_draft_sc_mpc_ext=>TY_FINAL, 
          ls_item type zcl_zgr_odata_draft_sc_mpc=>ts_so_item. 
  
  io_data_provider->read_entry_data( 
     IMPORTING 
       es_data = ls_final 
   ). 
  
  ls_item = CORRESPONDING #( ls_final ). 
  
  INSERT zgr_t_soi_draft FROM ls_item. 
  
  IF Sy-subrc EQ 0. 
  
    er_entity = ls_item. 
  
  ENDIF. 
  
  endmethod.

 

 

UPDATE Operation: 

Update operation for header and item entities will update the draft tables. 

Update Operation For Header Data:

 

 

  method SO_HEADERSET_UPDATE_ENTITY. 
  
    DATA: ls_final TYPE zcl_zgr_odata_draft_sc_mpc_ext=>ty_final, 
           ls_header TYPE zcl_zgr_odata_draft_sc_mpc_ext=>ts_so_header. 
  
    io_data_provider->read_entry_data( 
       IMPORTING 
         es_data = ls_final 
     ). 
  
    ls_header = CORRESPONDING #( ls_final ). 
  
    IF sy-subrc Eq 0. 
  
      MODIFY zgr_t_soh_draft FROM ls_header. 
  
        er_entity = ls_header. 
  
    ENDIF. 
  
  endmethod.

 

 

Update operation for Item data: 

 

 

 method SO_ITEMSET_UPDATE_ENTITY. 
  
    DATA: ls_final TYPE zcl_zgr_odata_draft_sc_mpc_ext=>ty_final, 
           ls_item TYPE zcl_zgr_odata_draft_sc_mpc_ext=>ts_so_item. 
  
    io_data_provider->read_entry_data( 
       IMPORTING 
         es_data = ls_final 
     ). 
  
    ls_item = CORRESPONDING #( ls_final ). 
  
    IF sy-subrc EQ 0. 
  
      MODIFY zgr_t_soi_draft FROM ls_item. 
  
    ENDIF. 
  
  endmethod.

 

 

DELETE Operation: 

In this case, a delete operation is not implemented as a Sales Order can not be deleted from the system. Usually, it will be set as all items rejected etc., which can be done in an update operation. 

Function Import Implementation: 

Method /IWBEP/IF_MGW_APPL_SRV_RUNTIME~EXECUTE_ACTION is redefined to implement the function imports. 

CreateDraft: 

  • Call the method Create_draft with the Sales order number. 

DeleteDraft: 

  • Delete the draft data from the header and item draft tables. 

SaveOrder: 

  • Check if the order number exists. If it does, it is a change mode, else it is a create mode. 
  • Get data from draft tables. 
  • Call create/change BAPI. 
  • Return the Message with order number. 

 

 

Function Import implementation:

 

 

  METHOD /iwbep/if_mgw_appl_srv_runtime~execute_action. 
  
    DATA : lv_vbeln_out TYPE vbeln. 
     DATA : ls_header_in TYPE bapisdhd1, 
            lt_items_in  TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF bapisditm, 
            lt_partners  TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF bapiparnr, 
            lt_return    TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF bapiret2. 
  
    CASE iv_action_name. 
       WHEN 'CreateDraft'. 
  
        TRY. 
  
          DATA(lt_pars) = io_tech_request_context->get_parameters( ). 
           DATA(lv_vbeln) = VALUE vbeln_va( lt_pars[ name = 'SALESORDER' ]-value OPTIONAL ). 
  
          lv_vbeln = |{ lv_vbeln ALPHA = IN }|. 
           DATA(ls_return) = create_draft( vbeln = lv_vbeln  ). 
  
        CATCH cx_sy_itab_line_not_found. 
  
          "Error 
  
          ls_return-test = 'E Invalid call'. 
  
        ENDTRY. 
  
        copy_data_to_ref( 
           EXPORTING 
             is_data = ls_return 
           CHANGING 
             cr_data = er_data 
         ). 
  
      WHEN 'DeleteDraft'. 
          
         TRY. 
           lt_pars = io_tech_request_context->get_parameters( ). 
           lv_vbeln = VALUE vbeln_va( lt_pars[ name = 'SALESORDER' ]-value ). 
           lv_vbeln = |{ lv_vbeln ALPHA = IN }|. 
           DELETE FROM zgr_t_soh_draft WHERE vbeln = _vbeln. 
           IF sy-subrc EQ 0. 
  
            DELETE FROM zgr_t_soi_draft WHERE vbeln = _vbeln. 
  
            ls_return-test = 'S draft deleted'. 
  
          ENDIF. 
  
        CATCH cx_sy_itab_line_not_found. 
           "Error 
           ls_return-test = 'E Invalid call'. 
  
        ENDTRY. 
         copy_data_to_ref( 
           EXPORTING 
             is_data = ls_return 
           CHANGING 
             cr_data = er_data 
         ). 
  
      WHEN 'SaveOrder'. 
  
        TRY. 
  
          lt_pars = io_tech_request_context->get_parameters( ). 
           lv_vbeln = VALUE vbeln_va( lt_pars[ name = 'SALESORDER' ]-value OPTIONAL ). 
           lv_vbeln = |{ lv_vbeln ALPHA = IN }|. 
  
          select SINGLE vbeln FROM vbak into (lv_vbeln_Test) 
                   WHERE vbeln = _vbeln. 
  
            IF sy-subrc NE 0. 
  
              CLEAR lv_vbeln_test. 
  
            ENDIF. 
  
            SELECT SINGLE FROM zgr_t_soh_draft 
                       FIELDS * 
                       WHERE vbeln = _vbeln 
                       INTO (ls_header). 
  
              IF sy-subrc NE 0. 
  
                ls_return-test = 'E Sales order not found'. 
  
                ELSE. 
  
                  "Fetch draft items 
  
                  SELECT FROM zgr_t_soi_draft 
                          FIELDS * 
                          WHERE vbeln = _vbeln 
                          INTO TABLE (lt_item). 
  
                    IF lv_vbeln_test IS NOT INITIAL. "Change mode 
  
                      DATA : ls_headerx_in TYPE bapisdh1x, 
                              ls_uheader_in TYPE bapisdhd1, 
                              lt_itemsx_in TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF bapisditmx. 
  
                      lt_items_in = VALUE #( FOR  ls_item IN lt_item 
                                           ( itm_number = ls_item-posnr 
                                             short_text = ls_item-arktx 
                                            ) 
                                           ). 
  
 
                      lt_itemsx_in = VALUE #( FOR ls_item IN lt_item 
                                            ( itm_number = ls_item-posnr 
                                              updateflag = 'U' 
                                              short_text = 'X' 
                                            ) 
                                           ). 
  
                      ls_headerx_in-updateflag = 'U'. 
                       lv_vbeln_out = lv_vbeln_test. 
  
                      CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_SALESORDER_CHANGE' 
                         EXPORTING 
                           salesdocument               = lv_vbeln_test 
                           order_header_inx            = ls_headerx_in 
                         TABLES 
                           return                      = lt_return 
                           ORDER_ITEM_IN               = lt_items_in 
                           ORDER_ITEM_INX              = lt_itemsx_in 
                                 . 
                      ELSE. 
  
                       ls_header_in-doc_type = 'ZOR1'. 
                        ls_header_in-doc_date = ls_header-erdat. 
                        ls_header_in-created_by = ls_header-ernam. 
                        ls_header_in-currency = ls_header-waerk. 
                        ls_header_in-sales_org = ls_header-vkorg. 
                        ls_header_in-distr_chan = ls_header-vtweg. 
                        ls_header_in-division = ls_header-spart. 
  
                       lt_partners = VALUE #( ( partn_role = 'AG' 
                                                 partn_numb = ls_header-kunnr ) ). 
                        lt_items_in = VALUE #( FOR LS_ITEM IN lt_item 
                                            ( itm_number = ls_item-posnr 
                                              material = ls_item-matnr 
                                              short_text = ls_item-arktx 
                                              target_qty = ls_item-zmeng 
                                              target_qu = ls_item-meins 
                                              plant = ls_item-werks 
                                              store_loc = ls_item-lgort 
                                              ship_point = ls_item-vstel 
                                              route = ls_item-route 
                                             ) 
                                            ). 
  
                       CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_SALESORDER_CREATEFROMDAT2' 
                          EXPORTING 
                            order_header_in               = ls_header_in 
                         IMPORTING 
                           SALESDOCUMENT                 = lv_vbeln_out 
                          tables 
                           RETURN                        = lt_return 
                           ORDER_ITEMS_IN                = lt_items_in 
                           order_partners                = lt_partners 
                                  . 
                     ENDIF. 
  
                    IF NOT line_exists( lt_return[ type = 'E' ] ). 
  
                      "Update FM to delete the draft 
                       CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT' 
 *                       EXPORTING 
 *                         WAIT          = 
 *                       IMPORTING 
 *                         RETURN        = 
                                 . 
  
                      "Delete Draft 
  
                      delete FROM zgr_t_soh_draft WHERE vbeln = lv_vbeln. 
                       delete FROM zgr_t_soi_draft WHERE vbeln = lv_vbeln. 
                       ls_return-test = lv_vbeln_out. 
  
                    ELSE. 
  
                      ls_return-test = 'E Error in Bapi Call'. 
  
                    ENDIF. 
  
              ENDIF. 
  
 
        CATCH cx_sy_itab_line_not_found. 
  
          "Error 
           ls_return-test = 'E Invalida call'. 
  
        ENDTRY. 
  
        me->copy_data_to_ref( 
           EXPORTING 
             is_data = ls_return 
           CHANGING 
             cr_data = er_data 
         ). 
     ENDCASE. 
   ENDMETHOD.

 

 

Steps to Register the Services: 

Goto SAP Gateway services (/IWFND/MAINT_SERVICES). 

 

Click on Add Services node. 

 

Provide the System Alias and Technical service name which is generated while generating the OData services. 

Click on Get services node. 

 

Select the services and click on Add Selected Services. 

 

Provide the Package name and click on continue. 

Select the services by using Filter. 

Click on SAP Gateway Client. 

Some basic scenarios output:

entry.title: Re: A new generation of CDS views: CDS view entities
entry.content:
Hi @ChristianGünter,

This issue is documented in SAP Note 3439871 - "ABAP CDS: Missing literal trimming in case expressions in view entities.". Please check the documented workaround. 

Sorry for the inconvenience and best regards, Joerg

entry.title: SAP Build App -BTP Connection to "XXX" established. Response returned: "401: Unauthorized"
entry.content:
I did this very nice video " Access Demo SAP APIs for SAP Build" (https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=11TUQgQi-9k) to create an app using a Business Partner sandbox API and worked fine.  I could see the data when lauching the web app. Really nice indeed!

 

But one day after I could no longer see the data when oppening web preview.

In the Build App, in the data section, I can see my integration (BTP destination), install it,  enable entities and when executing "browse real data" it works perfectly.

But when I make a binding with a data variable, and launch the web app, the data no longer appears.

One point is that, within the BTP, when I try to test the destination (check connection), the system returns a message of :  Connection to "demoApp_BP" established. Response returned: "401: Unauthorized"

I have already cleared the browser cache, reinstalled the Build App, deleted and created a new subaccount in the BTP, but none of this worked. 

OBS:  I put all the role colections on my btp user ( Custom IAS tenant and Default identity provider  users) and i m loggin in SAP Business Accelerator Hub

Could someone please help me?

I really can't see what is different from a day ago when the data was returned ok.

Thanks.

 

Here some of the app

when oppening web preview = no data

entry.title: Re: SAP Real Estate Management for Property Leasing: The Need to Comply with IFRS 16
entry.content: SAP Real Estate Management is a robust solution that helps streamline operations for real estate companies, offering comprehensive tools to manage properties, leases, and contracts efficiently. It provides powerful analytics and automation features, making property management easier and more transparent. Whether businesses are dealing with large portfolios or specific tasks like lease payments and contract renewals, SAP Real Estate Management simplifies the entire process. This system can be particularly valuable in high-demand markets such as NY rent own sell, where managing multiple transactions and properties requires effective tools to stay competitive.

entry.title: Re: Local Development Issue Once Multi-tenancy Added
entry.content:
We were able to answer this issue ourselves.  In order to continue working locally in memory as a single-tenant application, all we had to do was add this to cds.requires:

"[development]": {
        "db": {
          "credentials": {
            "url": ":memory:"
          }
        }
      }

We had tried something similar before, but had the url value wrong for memory.

entry.title: Re: B2B Integration Factory's Role and Value Adds in the B2B Cloud Integration journey
entry.content:
Hui @mert_oezkan ,

As requested by Gunther, I have submitted a request on https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/331262 and also have done the necessary steps on Github (you can refer our conversations in the previous blog).

Do I now also need to send a request to b2bintegrationfactory@sap.com ?

It would be great if SAP provides some dashboard to customers on the status such as "Accepted / In review / In development" etc so that we can inform our clients of the expectations (example to postpone the build in the current client project / build something custom using existing V2 package / build something temporary ) while we wait for the extension to be available as part of the standard package. This is just to manage expectations and not burn uncessary efforts if it is going to be considered as part of the standard going forward.

Thanks

Saurabh

entry.title: PDM Newsletter RISE with SAP - October 2024
entry.content:
Welcome to the PDM Newsletter RISE with SAP Blog!

We're excited to introduce you to our monthly newsletter, your go-to resource for staying connected with the latest innovations, insights, and trends in the RISE with SAP ecosystem. Each edition is thoughtfully curated to provide exclusive expert guidance, inspiring stories, and actionable content designed to help you enhance your skills and stay ahead in the ever-evolving world of SAP.

Whether you're looking to explore emerging technologies or gain valuable knowledge tailored to your needs, our newsletter has you covered. We also encourage you to share this resource with your network and become an active member of our vibrant partner community.

Thank you for your continued support! Should you have any feedback or questions, don’t hesitate to reach out. We’re here to help you make the most of your RISE with SAP journey.

Stay tuned, stay informed, and sign up today! 

Let's grow together!

 

Victor Echeverri Reyes                                                               

 SAP S/4HANA Cloud Partner Delivery Manager                                                                                   Ecosystem Delivery Success   
 E: victor.echeverri.reyes@sap.com

 

 

 

Top Highlights

Overview                                     

  Register/Access

SAP Enterprise Cloud Services – Partner Summit 2024 (EMEA/MEE)

 

SAP Enterprise Cloud Services Partner Summit 2024' is a virtual summit to empower our SAP PartnerEdge, Sell and SAP PartnerEdge, Service partners. It will be scenario-based training comprised of some of the topics listed below.

 

            Register

 

  • Session: 15th October 2024
    09:00 AM to 12:00 PM CET

 

  • Session: 16th  October 2024
    09:00 AM to 11:30 PM CET

 

 

Rise into the future: Make your move to an AI-infused cloud ERP

On October 22 join SAP leaders, customers, partners, and industry experts virtually to learn about the real-world benefits of cloud ERP and AI. It’s time for customers to embrace the AI future so their businesses can innovate faster, execute efficiently, and stay agile.

           Register

 

  • Session: 22nd October 2024
    08:00 AM to 11:00 AM CEST

      

SAP TechEd Virtual

Join us for a high-energy, interactive virtual event. Gain skills and insights, learn about best practices and customer successes, and connect with tech experts, developers, and IT leaders for a rich learning and community-building experience

 

            Register

 

  • Session: 8-9th October 2024

 

      

Partner-Led Territories

Partner-Led Territories (PLT) was launched in 2023 as a key initiative to scale and accelerate high volume cloud growth in select territories with our most mature partners. In 2024, PLT will be further expanded as regions and market units are identifying more areas to be covered by this model based on the market potential and anticipated impact. Participating partners are expected to drive customer lifetime value with minimal support from SAP across the entire Customer Value Journey.

            Access

 

 

ABAP Cloud Partner Certification Academy

Expand your current skills with our new ABAP Cloud Partner Certification Academy. It will let you learn about and get ready to certification for ABAP Cloud faster than ever before.

.

 

             Register

 

  • Session: 22nd – 23rd – 24th – 29th October 2024
    08:00 AM to 12:30 PM Europe

      

RISE with SAP Methodology Advisory

This service offers advisory on RISE with SAP Methodology and clean core for partners embarking on customer engagements involving a transformation and transition to RISE with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition via New Implementation or System Conversion.

             Access

SuccessConnect Virtual

 

SuccessConnect in 2024 will offer both an in-person and virtual experience. The official event dates are October 28–30, 2024. 

 

             Register

 

  • Session: 28th – 30th  October 2024

 

Events and Webinars for Partners

Overview

   Register/Access

Quarterly EMEA Partner Innovation Meeting Series

 

We are delighted to invite you to our next EMEA Partner Innovation Meeting on Wednesday 9th of October at 11 am CET. This will be our final chance to connect and discuss some important topics that will challenge, motivate and inspire us all to end 2024 on a high.

 

    Register

 

 

Partner enablement for seamless customer onboarding

In today’s competitive market, a smooth and successful implementation process is crucial for driving customer satisfaction and achieving long-term success. This session is designed to equip partners with the tools, knowledge, and best practices needed to guide customers through a seamless onboarding and implementation experience. Participants will learn how to anticipate and address common customer onboarding challenges. By empowering partners with the necessary tools and resources, we help ensure they are well-positioned to assist customers in achieving rapid success, building trust, and maximizing return on investment.

    Register

 

  • Session: 29th October 2024
    10:00 AM to 11:00 AM Americas

 

 

Deep insights on embedded launch activities for RISE with SAP customers

This session can provide the clear understanding of the onboarding process of RISE with SAP customers as well as the embedded launch activities process, documentations, resources and interlock with SAP Activate methodology

    Replay

Understand the SAP Business Accelerator Hub

The SAP Business Accelerator Hub is the place to discover business accelerator to speed up implementation but with nearly more than 34K individual artefacts, navigating the site can be daunting. I have seen it grow from a handful of packages to the what it is today, I would like to share my perspective on the website.

    Register

 

  • Session: 23rd October 2024
    08:00 AM to 09:00 AM Europe
  • Session: 23rd October 2024
    03:00 PM to 04:00 PM Europe

 

 

RISE with SAP add-on package for automotive

SAP is known for its depth and breadth of industry solutions. Since the early years of SAP ERP, SAP has enhanced the business capabilities provided by the core ERP with industry-specific capabilities that address the core business needs of companies in their respective industries.

 

  Register

 

  • Session: 18th September 2024
    03:30 PM to 04:30 PM CET

 

ERP-Centric EPM – transform how you face the future based on granular, reliable, instant insights

 

SAP’s ERP-Centric approach to SAP Enterprise Performance Management is designed the way it is to help companies move their business forward based on instant, multi-dimensional insights to maximize financial performance while optimize the tax and sustainability footprint at the same time. It takes out outdated silos of statutory accounting, management controlling and corporate tax management with one integrated approach based on unique transactional truth.

 

  Register

 

  • Session: 9th October 2024
    08:00 AM to 09:00 AM CET
  • Session: 9th October 2024
    11:00 AM to 12:00 PM EST

Expand the value of RISE with SAP with add-on packages for chief financial officer and chief supply chain officer

 

SAP highly values the commitment of our partner community for the success of our RISE with SAP strategy. Our customers need to understand the full value of our suite of private cloud solutions. For our partner community, it is important to position all SAP product bundles precisely while highlighting the benefits of SAP’s cloud-based AI-enabled suite of solutions

 

  Register

 

  • Session: 11th October 2024
    09:00 AM to 10:00 AM America

 

Rise into the future: Managed cloud services and security in RISE with SAP

 

RISE with SAP includes an AI-enabled cloud ERP that’s managed and optimized by SAP. A primary benefit for IT is to let SAP experts handle the day-to-day technical management of your cloud ERP infrastructure. Join us in a 30-minute webinar to hear RISE with SAP can help your IT team.

 

    Register

 

  • Session: 10th September 2024
    11:00 AM to 11:30 AM CEST

 

Masterclass for Customer Success on SAP’s cloud security and data protection

This session is designed to provide an overview of the tools and presentation material to confidently answer customer concerns around cloud security and customer data protection across SAP’s solution portfolio and instill trust in the customer’s cloud business transformation with SAP.

 

    Register

 

  • Session 1: 5th  September 2024
    09:00 AM to 09:45 AM CEST
  • Session 2: 5th  September 2024
    04:00 PM to 04:45 PM CEST

Enhancing Support Quality, Troubleshooting, and Collaboration with SAP

 

For a superior customer experience and to mitigate unnecessary delay and disruption, partners should use own users when engaging SAP support on customer behalf. Customers and partners are recommended to use the service partner user management application to authorize partner’s S-user ID(s) in SAP for Me on their behalf. Learn more about this application and excel in support-related aspects.

 

    Access

 

 

 

Business AI

Overview

Register/Access

Explore Generative AI Hub with SAP AI Core and SAP AI Launchpad

SAP AI Core is a service in the SAP Business Technology Platform that helps customers and partners deploy and run AI models at scale without compromising data privacy and cost-effectiveness. It provides seamless integration with SAP solutions and any AI scenario can be easily realized using open-source frameworks

 

   Access

SAP AI Core

SAP AI Core helps you to develop and productize AI functions with out-of-the-box integration with SAP solutions.

   Access

 

Generative AI Hub

The generative AI hub incorporates generative AI into your AI activities in SAP AI Core and SAP AI Launchpad

 Access

 

Augment Your SAP BTP Use Cases With AI Foundation on SAP BTP

AI Foundation is the starting point for SAP developers to build and extend AI applications. It includes everything you need from easily embedding business-ready AI into your application, to building your own custom AI models from scratch.

  Replay

 

 

Infuse Generative AI into your SAP BTP Based Extensions

SAP Business AI is SAP’s answer to infuse the power of Artificial Intelligence into SAP and partner applications, including the recent breakthroughs in the space of generative AI. SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) provides the underlying AI Foundation, that SAP and our partner ecosystem can benefit from in order to build innovative AI-infused extensions and applications.

  Replay

 

 

 

Certification for Partner AI Applications on SAP BTP available for FREE

SAP Integration and Certification Center (SAP ICC) is committed to support partners in the adoption of SAP Business AI and build innovate AI Apps. To further assist in these efforts, we are excited to introduce a limited-time promotion, providing free certification for partner AI applications until the end of 2024, as long as the application meets the certification requirements.

 

   Access

RISE with SAP Transformation

Overview

  Register/Access

Competency & Specialization

 

The competency framework evolves SAP’s recognition of its partner ecosystem expertise by categorizing partners by the competency and specialization they offer the market giving customers transparency on the skills, expertise, and maturity of a partner’s SAP practice. The competencies and specializations are aligned to SAP’s cloud portfolio and are cloud-focused.

 

   Access

Requirements for the Competency Framework

 

Partner performance and achievement are key drivers for attaining a competency or specialization designations. The requirements include three key pillar areas: Knowledge, Customer Success and Proficiency.

   Access

Explore SAP for Professional Services

 

Deliver client value and sustain growth for professional services. Meet client expectations, generate new revenue streams, offer digital services, and adopt new outcome-based business models for nonlinear growth by integrating data and processes through digital transformation and managing your total workforce in the cloud.

 

  Access

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Sales Tools & Assets

 

Access the SAP S/4HANA Cloud sales resources, success stories, and competitive insights to succeed in the field.

 

   Access

SAP Introduces a New Validation Recognition for Service Partners

To support customers’ RISE with SAP transformations, SAP is training and validating partners to use RISE with SAP Methodology and collaborating closely with these partners on quality and consistency. By following the methodology, customers can drive predictable timelines for implementations and provide progress transparency with key milestone assessments.

 

    Access

Customer Success Excellence Hub

 

Customer success is partner success. SAP and partners together delivering customer success. Customer success is all about creating the best customer experience – supporting customers to continuously realize value from their SAP cloud investment, delivering a simple consistent experience across SAP and partners. Customer success is a key part of everyone’s role – from sales through to service and support. Investing in customer success is an investment in growth.

 

    Access

Guide: RISE with SAP - Qualified Partner Cloud Architect Access Management Request

This asset enables partners on how to raise a request for system/tool access to key private cloud process tools, including SAP Work Zone and the RISE with SAP pre-provisioning form BTP application

 

    Access

News from SAP Activate - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408, RISE with SAP, and more

We are pleased to announce the availability of the updates to SAP Activate. This release significantly enhances the methodology guidance across a wide range of products and solution-specific content. These updates aim to streamline and improve the implementation process, providing a more seamless experience for our valued users. Here are the key highlights for this release. You can learn more in the detailed sections below.

 

 

     Access

RISE with SAP: Qualification and recognition for partners

Enhance your skills, increase your presence, and solidify your credibility in the marketplace by achieving designation and recognition available to SAP partners who deliver implementation services for RISE with SAP.

 

     Access

SAP Private Cloud Metering

SAP Private Cloud Metering provides full transparency on the SAP Private Cloud consumption with monthly updated metering data. The metering data is made available in SAP for Me so that license gaps can be identified and clarified in a timely and product-specific manner.

 

     Access

SAP S/4HANA Customer Evolution Program

 

The SAP S/4HANA Customer Evolution Program is designed to help our ERP installed base customers accelerate their move to SAP S/4HANA Cloud. It comprises an end-to-end methodology providing prescriptive guidance, best practices, and automated tools and more to simplify our customer’s transition to SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

 

     Access

Transition Brownfield Projects

This Knowledge Base Document is the central reference to additional documents focused on guidelines and information for Brownfield Projects (Migration/ Conversion or System Copy) in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition.

 

      Access

      

RISE with SAP adoption framework

 

Use this pitch deck to present RISE with SAP adoption framework to your customers. The RISE with SAP adoption framework provides clarity on the entire cloud journey and brings partners and SAP together to collaborate under a single cloud operational governance model to proactively manage our common success.

 

 

    Access

 

Customer Onboarding Webcast

Select the solution of interest to access live and on demand webinars tailored to simplify your journey with SAP.

  • SAP Cloud ERP
  • SAP BTP
  • SAP SuccessFactors HCM
  • SAP Financial Management
  • SAP Training and Adoption
  • SAP Signavio and LeanIX
  • SAP Intelligent Spend and Business Network
  • SAP Supply Chain Management

    Access

 

Signavio

Overview

Register/Access

What's New in business process transformation

Learn more about Business Process Transformation latest news, recently added, and/or updated information. 

 

Access

Onboarding Resource Center: One-Stop-Shop for SAP Signavio

 

If you are a new SAP Signavio customer, it can be overwhelming to find your bearings around the variety of SAP websites, documentation and platforms. With the huge number of texts, videos and other materials available on SAP Signavio, it can be hard to find the right information. If you can find it at all.  That’s the reason we have created the Onboarding Resource Center (ORC), to give you a single access point to find all relevant resources and information about SAP Signavio – well-structured and at a glance. 

 

 Access

Exceed on procurement excellence with SAP Signavio and SAP Ariba

In today's rapidly changing business landscape, intelligent procurement has emerged as a critical component for ensuring business resilience. Achieving this requires a strong focus on processes. Join us for an insightful webcast where SAP Ariba and SAP Signavio come together to demonstrate how their combined strengths provide the fastest path to procurement excellence. Discover how a value-driven approach to process harmonization can elevate your procurement strategy and drive sustainable success

 

 Register

 

  • Session: 23rd October 2024
    09:00 AM

SAP Signavio Lab Space - Business Process Transformation Innovation

Discover the power of SAP Signavio Lab Space and learn how this innovation platform can contribute to a better SAP Signavio. This webcast will showcase how you can effectively engage with the platform and explore its experimental features. 

Register

    04:00 PM CEST

Navigating DORA Compliance - Achieving Digital Resilience with SAP LeanIX and SAP Signavio - US/EMEA

In this webcast, we will demonstrate how SAP LeanIX and SAP Signavio can help you meet the requirements of the Digital Operational Resilience Act (DORA) more efficiently and effectively. Learn how our integrated solutions support your efforts to ensure compliance and strengthen operational resilience.

 

Register

 

  • Session: 17th October 2024
    04:00 PM CEST

 

Explore SAP Signavio Process Transformation Manager

SAP Signavio Process Transformation Manager is a cloud-based process transformation solution that gives companies the ability to collaboratively plan, manage, and execute transformation initiatives leading to quantifiable success. The comprehensive solution allows organizations to benefit from centralized and collaborative enhancements, make value-driven decisions, and achieve measurable business value through continuous improvements

 

Access

SAP Value Lifecycle Manager and SAP Signavio – the next level of value selling

Understand your customers’ objectives and challenges by taking a customer-centric value selling approach. Learn about integrating SAP Signavio Process Insights, discovery edition with the SAP Value Lifecycle Manager business case to effectively assess customer processes and business maturity, as well as calculate potential business benefits.

 

 Register

 

  • Session: 1st  October 2024
    04:00 PM to 1:00 AM CET

 

SAP LeanIX | Cloud Test, Demo, and Development

 

SAP LeanIX is a market leader for enterprise architecture, driving the modernization of IT landscapes and continuous business transformation.

 

 Access

 

 

Link the SAP Signavio Process Manager to the SAP Solution Manager - Hands on session

This webinar is a follow up to the September webinar on the interface between SAP Signavio Process Manager and SAP Solution Manager - the Business Process Model Connector. In the earlier webinar we gave an overview on the general concepts. In this hands on webinar we will focus on the actual doing in the system with an extensive demo and tips and tricks. This will help partners to leverage the existing documentation and to implement this connection. The whole session is hands on and interactive. Target group are SAP partners working with SAP Signavio and SAP Solution Manager. Focus is on delivery.

 

 Register

 

  • Session: 29th October 2024
    04:00 PM to 05:00 PM CEST

 

Explore Business Process Transformation

 

Design, Analyze and Automate improved Processes — Driving Efficiency and Customer Excellence with SAP Signavio. SAP offers a holistic suite of flexible process transformation solutions for customers to transform their business processes end-to-end with SAP Signavio solutions.

 

Access

How to use the new SAP Signavio Process Insights connector (SAP Signavio plug and gain eebinar series)

In the session, you will receive a better understanding of the newly available connector for plug and gain, enabling partners and customers to load and refresh data individually. We will outline the connector’s objectives and value proposition along with providing a detailed demo on how to actually navigate and use the connector. We will also provide additional sources, e.g.  blog entries and videos, for deeper knowledge and self-enablement.

 

 Register

 

  • Session: 10th October 2024
    04:00 PM to 05:00 PM CET

 

Unlock the secret to successful RISE with SAP Cross LoB selling and enjoy increased revenues, profits, and customer loyalty.

 

Join this enablement session to learn how the SAP Customer Evolution cross-solution area approach, resources, and tools, along with the SAP Signavio Process Transformation Suite will elevate your sales engagements to a new level of productivity.

 Register

 

  • Session: 2nd  October 2024
    09:00 AM to 10:00 AM CET – 04:00 PM to 05:00 PM CET

 

Partner Delivery Groups

Overview

Register/Access

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition

This group focuses on providing enhanced learnings and periodic updates to the partner community with respect to the SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition product. Our goal is to facilitate collaboration with your peers and SAP subject matter experts.

 

Access

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

 

This private group is focused solely on helping you make your SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition projects successful. SAP Business Technology Platform

Get up to speed with the latest content and activities in the SAP BTP Partner Group. Don’t miss out the upcoming SAP BTP Partner Community Call on September 20th. Get all the details, save your seat today and share with your network

 

Access

 

SAP AI Core Community

Confidently deploy and integrate AI models designed for SAP applications, cost-efficiently at scale while preserving privacy and compliance.

Access

SAP Ariba and SAP Business Network

In the SAP Ariba and Business Network Delivery Group , we excited to share that we are welcoming our Test, Demonstration & Development Program team, to the SAP Ariba and SAP Business Network Partner Delivery Group. This collaboration has allowed us to invite over 300 partner consultants who participate in the Test, Demonstration & Development Program. This initiative is key to enhancing our partner experiences and fostering stronger collaboration. We are extending open invitations to our ISBN partners to ask their how-to questions, with a view to generate constructive and continuous learning in a growing partner group within SAP Community

Access

SAP Signavio Community

Connect with other SAP Signavio users or experts, find blog posts and ask your questions to the Signavio community.

 

Access

 

SAP SuccessFactors HXM

SAP SuccessFactors is delivering value across multiple types of Artificial Intelligence (AI).

  • Generative AI: Accelerate HR processes and tasks by quickly generating or summarizing text across the SAP SuccessFactors HXM suite.
  • Conversational AI: Deliver a better employee experience while lowering personnel costs with a natural language processing chatbot.
  • Automation: Lower personnel costs while increasing data accuracy and accelerating repetitive core HR processes.
  • Deep Learning AI: Transform talent development and employee experience with personalized recommendations and deep insights.

 

Access

SAP Cloud ALM Community

 

Follow the SAP Cloud ALM Community to keep you up to date and ask questions

 

Access

SAP Customer Experience & CRM

On regular cadences, we partner with internal Customer Experience & CRM subject matter experts to share best practices, new product information and thought leadership through delivery blogs in our partner delivery group. Our blogs are amplified through targeted communications and social media to help encourage engagement amongst partners and internal subject matter experts. Internal subject matter experts are always welcome to contribute blogs amongst any of our other solution partner delivery groups as well.

 

Access

entry.title: Export and Import Software Collections in a 3-System Landscape for SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Dear Community,

Image/data in this blog is from SAP internal systems, sample data, or demo systems. Any resemblance to real data is purely coincidental.

Overview:

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud, the 3-system landscape consists of three core systems and four tenants, each serving a unique role in the development and deployment process:

Development System:

  • Development tenant (for developer extensibility)
  • Customizing tenant (for business configuration and key user extensibility)

Test System:

  • Test tenant (for validation and testing)

Production System:

  • Production tenant (for live, productive usage)

In a 3-system landscape, you have a development system, test system, and production system. Since development and testing activities are separated into two dedicated systems, it is possible to have advanced development projects in this landscape, enabling developer extensibility options. For more information, see System Landscapes in SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

Configuration content in the 3-system landscape is provided via SAP Central Business Configuration, which is connected only to the development system. The content updates and upgrades in all systems are handled differently from the software updates and upgrades

Overview of Software and Content Upgrade | SAP Help Portal

  • Working with Content | SAP Help Portal
  • Upgrade in Detail | SAP Help Portal
  • Transports | SAP Help Portal
  • Transport Automation | SAP Help Portal
  • Best Practices | SAP Help Portal

Development System

    • Development Tenant: Developer Extensibility
      • What is Developer Extensibility? Developer extensibility allows developers to create and manage development projects in SAP S/4HANA Cloud using ABAP Development Tools (ADT). Changes made through ADT are recorded in workbench requests.
        • How transport management works in Developer Extensibility:
          • As a developer, you implement custom ABAP code in the development tenant using ABAP Development Tools (ADT). For instance, you may create a custom business object using the ABAP RESTful application programming model.
          • All the changes are recorded on workbench requests, once you have finished the implementation, you can release the developer extensibility transport via ADT (ABAP Development Tools) through the Transport Organizer view in ADT (ABAP Development Tool), For more info See Transport Organizer.  
          • An administrator imports the developer extensibility transport into the test and production tenants using the Import Collection app in SAP Fiori. For more information about your system landscape and change recording, see 3-System Landscape and Transport Management and Change Recording and Transportation. 
    • Customizing Tenant: Business Configuration and Key User Extensibility
      • Business Configuration: Business configuration changes are handled through SAP Central Business Configuration (CBC). This involves scoping, organizational, or country-specific configurations.
        • When you have made your configuration changes, you release the corresponding transport requests by using the app Export Customizing Transports.
        • The SAP content update is merged with your custom configuration content in your SAP Central Business Configuration workspace, and the delta is automatically deployed to the customizing tenant in the development system, and it is – by default – automatically released, this transport request appears as collection in the SAP Fiori app Export Customizing Transports with status “released” under the name “BC Upgrade”
          • How transport management works for Business Configuration.
            • After completing project scoping in SAP CBC, a transport request is generated when the workspace is deployed to the customizing tenant.
            • The transport request is visible in the Export Customizing Transports app.
            • Take over the ownership of a request with the type of Central Business Configuration by selecting it and selecting Assign To Me. 

               

            • Note : All Tasks of the request type Central Business Configuration must have the status released. For more info refer Transport Types for Business Configurations and Managing Customizing Requests
  • Key User Extensibility Transport Process: Key user extensibility allows non-technical users to customize applications (e.g., adding fields or modifying UIs) based on business needs.
          • How transport management works for Key User Extensibility:
            • Create a software collection using the Export Software Collection app and assign your extension items to it.
            • Export the collection to transport the extension items.
            • Import the collection into the test tenant via the Import Collection app.For more detailed steps, refer to How to Manage and Export Your Software Collection | SAP Help Portal

Test System

Once all transport requests from the development system (customizing requests, software collections, and workbench requests) are exported needs to import to Test System using the Import Collection App.

Below are the actions that can occur, and which status is the initial status of an action in a collection version, and which status is the result of an action in a collection version.

  • Import -> Imports the collection version into the current system
  • Forward -> Makes the collection version available for import in the production system. This action is only available in the test system
  • Discard -> Discards a collection version so that it can’t be imported into the current system, nor can it be forwarded for import into a subsequent system. A discarded collection version is automatically transported to all subsequent systems so that any dependencies on the current version no longer exist. Key User Extensibility and CBC collection versions can’t be discarded.
  • Preimport -> The import validation has errors due to dependencies. You can decide to skip these errors and import your collection version without dependencies. Key User Extensibility and CBC collection versions can’t be preimported.
  • Set as Imported   - This status is set if the error is resolved locally in the import system

For more info, you can refer Importing a Software Collection | SAP Help Portal

  • Customizing Requests: Use the Import Collection app to import released customizing requests from the development system.
  • Developer Extensibility: Release the transport request, workbench request, developer extensibility request from ADT and import it into the test system using the Import Collection app.
  • Key User Extensibility: Open the Import Collection app, select the software collection, and import the extension items into the test tenant.
    Procedure to Import the collection to test system
    1. Open the Import Collection app.
    2. Select Collection type from the Collection Type drop-down.
    3. Select the collection you want to import

Once testing is completed and everything works as expected, the collections can be forwarded to the production system for final deployment. In a 3-system landscape, you can choose to transport your software collections automatically. 

      Transport Automation

                     In the Import Settings, choose the software collections you want to maintain the automation settings for. You can also choose to maintain the automation settings for SAP Scheduled Collections: These are collections scheduled by SAP, such as product-relevant updates that are in specific transports to reach the production system. Choose on which days you want the transport automation to be activated. Then, choose the earliest start time for your import. You can determine the number of hours within which the automated import should start. The start of the automated import depends on the system utilization. At the end of the automated import time range, the import process of collections won't end immediately. It'll still finish running the imports but not start new ones from the transport queue.

For more info, you can refer the below help document Transport Automation | SAP Help Portal

Production System

After successful testing, the transport requests are forwarded to the production system. The process is as follows:

  • Customizing Requests, Software Collection, and Workbench Requests, CBC requests are forwarded from the test tenant using the Import Collection app.
  • Once forwarded, you can import them into the production tenant through the Import Collection app.

For more details, refer Importing a Software Collection to the P-System.

Conclusion
The transport process in a 3-system landscape for SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud ensures a smooth transition of software collections, business configurations, and extensibility changes from the development environment through to the production environment. Understanding the flow of workbench requests, customizing requests, and software collections helps maintain system integrity and ensures that all changes are appropriately tested before going live.

For 3-System Landscape related to Extensibility and Transport Management, you can refer to the following blogs and KBAs:-

  • How to Manage and Move Transport Request in S/4HAN... - SAP Community

entry.title: Re: ML_GET_BLOCKED_INFO
entry.content:
It sounds possible that after the upgrade, the procedure definition is out of date and some active database connections are still using the old procedure definition until they are restarted. Have you tried running a recompile on the procedure in Oracle instead of restarting the entire database to see if that helps?

 

ALTER PROCEDURE ML_GET_BLOCKED_INFO COMPILE;

entry.title: Re: One Sold to party and multiple payers in S/4HANA
entry.content: You can't have multiple payer partners for one document, so you would need to look at billing split criteria or additional partner types to represent secondary 'RG' entries in a single invoice document.

entry.title: It’s All About BRIM: Join This Hybrid Event to Learn More About Billing and Revenue Management
entry.content:
If you know your way around SAP, you know there’s lots of abbreviations! But have you ever heard of BRIM? No? Then don’t worry – we’ve got you covered! Read on to learn more about BRIM and what it can do for you.

First things first. BRIM stands for “Billing and Revenue Innovation Management”. AS “Everything as a Service” is shaping the future of the global economy, companies of all sizes, from large multi-nationals to those in the rapidly growing mid-market, are evaluating their pricing models to create scalable, recurring revenues that require a shift towards long- term customer relationships. At the same time, consumers expect flexible, personalized offerings catered to them. 

Join us onsite or virtually to learn more about BRIM 

And here’s where you come in. To learn more about BRIM, you can join our hybrid SAP Billing and Revenue Innovation Management (BRIM) Forum on October 22 and 23, 2024. If you want to join virtually, you can do so via SAP Live Class. If you want to attend onsite, we’re looking forward to welcoming you onsite at the Training Center in Walldorf, Germany.  

Here’s what you can expect from the event: 

  • Benefit from first-hand expert knowledge and take the opportunity to exchange ideas with speakers from the SAP Product Management team. 
  • Listen to an exciting keynote from SAP executives on product strategies and roadmap. 
  • Join expert talks and dedicated training sessions including theoretical introductions, system demonstrations, and Q&A sessions. 
  • Meet our experts to discuss and clarify your specific questions. 
  • Engage in panel discussions on current strategic topics most relevant to your organization. 
  • Don’t neglect the entertainment factor and enjoy an evening with us in a relaxed atmosphere to expand your network. 

If you’re part of a project team, a consultant, key user, decision maker or manager – this event is for you. There’re no prerequisites, but basic knowledge in the field is recommended. Sign up today to bring out the best in you and your organization!

entry.title: Re: Can you remember the first time you heard of SAP
entry.content: I am a construction engineer by education, and my story with SAP began recently, when I lost my job as a construction manager. I learned about SAP a year ago (2023) and after the 6-month course, I passed the SAP MM and SAP SD exams. I live in Berlin but I was born in Poland. I try to know the work in Belin as a SAP Advisor and specialize in data research. I even went to interviews at BMW, but they said that they should look for a person who knows how to migrate data from SAP to S/4HANA, which I didn't have during the course. I would like to specialize in querying data from SAP to S/4HANA, but in the contract form per day. I would like to find a boot for automatic knowledge of about 12,000-13,000 pieces of data in a short time. I am looking for an internship as a junior SAP advisor and I would like to work with someone who knows about data migration from SAP to S/4HANA SAP. Have a nice day everyone.

entry.title: Re: 🟡 Petoberfest 2024
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

This is an (very, very) old one. Never leave your laptop unattended! 😉

entry.title: 🟡 Techtoberfest 2024
entry.content:
Last week was all about pets, this week is all about tech setups! Use this opportunity to show off your coding setup. This could be your desk, office, or even your favorite development tools (I personally want to see everyone’s favorite VS Code extensions).

To participate, you only need to complete the following steps.

Step 1: Take a picture or screenshot of your development setup. This could be your desk with your favorite knickknacks, your office and how you arrange your room for various purposes, your computer screen showing how you arrange your windows for optimal coding efficiency, or even just your favorite coding extensions or tools that help you develop better code.

 Step 2: Post that picture in the comments of this discussion post before EOD October 6th. If you’ve already posted the picture elsewhere, feel free to link that in the comments.

Step 3 (optional): Peruse the rest of the pictures to get ideas on how you might want to update your own setup.

Be as creative or relaxed as you want, the only thing that’s important is that you have fun.

Personally, I like to have a mobile setup so I can work from whichever seat the wind blows me to that day. I went ahead and shared four of my favorite VS Code extensions in the comments below so check that out if you need some inspiration.

Note: You are not restricted to only post during Friday, October 4th. This is open the entire second week of Devtoberfest, from September 30th through October 6th. We will award points and badges after that time.

Have fun and show us how you code!

entry.title: Re: 🟡 Techtoberfest 2024
entry.content: I want to share some of my favorite VS Code extensions. Toggle Quotes is so I can easily toggle between the different quotes “ ‘ `, TODO Highlight, because I like to write todos as I code and unless they are highly visible, I will accidentally push them to production, Trailing Spaces so I can see any extra whitespaces after the ends of lines because I have a cat who will walk on my keyboard and add 15 spaces at the end of a line without me knowing, and Color Highlight because I am a frontend developer who isn’t good enough to know what color the random 6 digit hex code is.
